title: viola adunca subsp. uncinulata
intro: Viola adunca subsp. uncinulata is a subspecies of the Western blue violet, a perennial flowering plant native to North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Viola adunca subsp. uncinulata has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, sore throats, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can also help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Viola adunca subsp. uncinulata can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
